prerequisites: A LinkedIn account that holds an ADMINISTRATOR role on the target LinkedIn Page (organization), since many Organization APIs require the authenticated member to have the ADMINISTRATOR role type.
A registered application in the LinkedIn Developer Portal to obtain a Client ID and Client Secret from the app's Auth tab.
An app product (requested under the Products tab) that grants the organization permissions r_organization_admin or rw_organization_admin so the app can read a Page and its reporting data.
The numeric organization ID of the LinkedIn Page (org_id), used to form the URN urn:li:organization:{id}.
An OAuth access token (and refresh token) generated for the app via LinkedIn's 3-legged Authorization Code flow.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 (3-legged Authorization Code flow)
authMethods.summary: LinkedIn Pages authenticates with OAuth 2.0. Register an app in the LinkedIn Developer Portal to get a client_id and client_secret, send the member through the authorization code flow to obtain an authorization code, then exchange that code at https://www.linkedin.com/oauth/v2/accessToken for an access_token and refresh_token. Access tokens are issued with a 60-day lifespan, so the refresh_token is used to renew access without re-prompting the member. These map to the connector secret fields credentials.client_id, credentials.client_secret, credentials.access_token, and credentials.refresh_token.

setupSteps.title: Create and configure a LinkedIn Developer application
setupSteps.body: In the LinkedIn Developer Portal, create a new app or select an existing one. Open the Auth tab to read the app's Client ID and Client Secret, and add an HTTPS redirect (callback) URL. Keep the Client Secret secure and never pass it in a URL.

setupSteps.title: Request organization API access
setupSteps.body: Under the app's Products tab, request a product that grants the organization permissions. To read a Page and its reporting data (follower, visitor, and content analytics), request the r_organization_admin scope. rw_organization_admin additionally allows managing organization pages and is restricted to organizations in which the authenticated member has the ADMINISTRATOR role type.

setupSteps.title: Generate OAuth tokens
setupSteps.body: Run LinkedIn's 3-legged OAuth Authorization Code flow (or use the Developer Portal token generator) to consent to the requested scopes. Exchange the returned authorization code at https://www.linkedin.com/oauth/v2/accessToken to receive an access_token and refresh_token. Provide these along with client_id and client_secret as the connector's credentials.

setupSteps.title: Provide the organization ID and run options
setupSteps.body: Set org_id to the numeric ID of the LinkedIn Page you administer (it forms urn:li:organization:{id}). Optionally set start_date to bound how far back data is pulled and time_granularity_type to control the reporting time bucket used by the connector's statistics streams.
